questions.question: It was Sunday on January 1, 2006. What was the day of the week January 1, 2010?
questions.explanation: On 31st December, 2005 it was Saturday. Number of odd days from the year 2006 to the year 2009 = [1 + 1 + 2 + 1] = 5 days. On 31stDecember 2009, it was Thursday. Thus, on 1stJan, 2010 it is Friday.

questions.question: What is the meaning of ‘T’ in SRESHTA Scheme launched by Govt. of India? > भारत सरकार द्वारा शुरू की गई SRESHTA योजना में 'T' का क्या अर्थ है?
questions.explanation: The Scheme for Residential Education for Students in High Schools in Targeted Areas [SHRESHTA] was launched by the government of India. The scheme aims to provide seats in private residential schools to meritorious students from Scheduled Castes [SCs]. Nodal ministry for this scheme is Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment. > लक्षित क्षेत्रों में हाई स्कूलों में छात्रों के लिए आवासीय शिक्षा योजना {Scheme for Residential Education for Students in High Schools in Targeted Areas [SHRESHTA]} भारत सरकार द्वारा शुरू की गई थी। इस योजना का उद्देश्य अनुसूचित जाति [एससी] के मेधावी छात्रों को निजी आवासीय विद्यालयों में सीटें प्रदान करना है। इस योजना के लिए नोडल मंत्रालय सामाजिक न्याय और अधिकारिता मंत्रालय है।

questions.question: Who was the first Deputy Chairman of Planning Commission? > योजना आयोग के पहले उपाध्यक्ष कौन थे?
questions.explanation: Sh Gulzarilal Nanda served as the first deputy chairman of the planning commission from 17 February 1953 until 21 September 1963. He was also served as the first and only acting Prime Minister of India. > श्री गुलजारीलाल नंदा ने 17 फरवरी 1953 से 21 सितंबर 1963 तक योजना आयोग के पहले उपाध्यक्ष के रूप में कार्य किया। उन्होंने भारत के पहले और एकमात्र कार्यवाहक प्रधान मंत्री के रूप में भी कार्य किया।

questions.question: What is the SI Unit of Magnetic Flux? > चुंबकीय प्रवाह की SI इकाई क्या है?
questions.explanation: Weber is SI Unit of Magnetic Flux. Tesla is SI Unit of Magnetic Flux Density. Henry is SI Unit of Electric inductance. Farade is SI Unit of Electrical Capacitance. > वेबर चुंबकीय प्रवाह की SI इकाई है। टेस्ला चुंबकीय प्रवाह घनत्व की SI इकाई है। हेनरी विद्युत प्रेरण की SI इकाई है। फराडे विद्युत धारिता की SI इकाई है।

questions.question: One of the following compounds gives a white precipitate with aqueous AgNO3 and a green colour in flame test. The compound is? > निम्नलिखित में से एक यौगिक जलीय AgNO3 के साथ सफ़ेद अवक्षेप और ज्वाला परीक्षण में हरा रंग देता है। वह यौगिक कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Above given all option give a white precipitation with aqueous AgNO3 but they give different colours in Flame test. Silver nitrate [AgNO3] reacts with halide ions [like Cl−] to form a white precipitate of silver halides [AgCl]. This reaction helps identify the presence of chloride ions in a solution. Example reaction: Cl− + AgNO3 → AgCl [white precipitate] + NO3− Flame tests are used to identify the presence of certain metal ions based on the color they produce when heated in a flame. Barium ions [Ba2+] produce a characteristic green flame, Sodium [yellow], Potassium [lilac], and Calcium [brick-red]. Conclusion: The compound that gives a white precipitate with aqueous AgNO3 and a green flame test is: BaCl2 > ऊपर दिए गए सभी विकल्प जलीय AgNO3 के साथ एक सफेद निक्षेप देते हैं लेकिन वे ज्वाला परीक्षण में अलग-अलग रंग देते हैं। सिल्वर नाइट्रेट [AgNO3] हैलाइड आयनों [जैसे Cl−] के साथ प्रतिक्रिया करके सिल्वर हैलाइड्स [AgCl] का एक सफेद अवक्षेप बनाता है। यह प्रतिक्रिया एक समाधान में क्लोराइड आयनों की उपस्थिति की पहचान करने में मदद करती है। उदाहरण प्रतिक्रिया: Cl− + AgNO3 → AgCl [सफेद अवक्षेप] + NO3− ज्वाला परीक्षणों का उपयोग कुछ धातु आयनों की उपस्थिति की पहचान करने के लिए किया जाता है, जो एक ज्वाला में गर्म होने पर उत्पन्न रंग के आधार पर होता है। बेरियम आयन [Ba2+] एक विशिष्ट हरी लौ, सोडियम [पीला], पोटेशियम [बकाइन], और कैल्शियम [लाल] उत्पन्न करते हैं। निष्कर्ष: वह यौगिक जो जलीय AgNO3 और हरी ज्वाला परीक्षण के साथ एक सफेद अवक्षेप देता है: BaCl2।

questions.question: When was first synchronized Census conducted? > पहली बार समन्वित जनगणना कब आयोजित की गई थी?
questions.explanation: The first census in India was conducted in 1872 at that time viceroy of India was Lord Mayo, and the first official and synchronized census which was conducted in 1881 and covered the entire continent of British India [except Kashmir] under Viceroy Lord Rippon. > भारत में पहली जनगणना 1872 में हुई थी, उस समय भारत के वायसराय लॉर्ड मेयो थे, और पहली आधिकारिक और समन्वित जनगणना 1881 में हुई थी और इसमें वायसराय लॉर्ड रिपन के अधीन ब्रिटिश भारत के पूरे महाद्वीप [कश्मीर को छोड़कर] को शामिल किया गया था।

questions.question: Which is the oldest Dam in India? > भारत का सबसे पुराना बांध कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: The kallanai Dam [the Grand Anicut Dam] is the oldest dam in India. It was built by King Karikala of the Chola dynasty. Other important Dam in India:- 1. Bhakra Nangal Dam Located in Himachal Pradesh and Punjab, this dam is the largest dam in India and second largest in Asia. 2. Hirakud Dam Located in Odisha, this dam is the longest man-made dam in the world. 3. Sardar Sarovar Dam Located in Gujarat, this dam is part of the Narmada Valley Project. 4. Nagarjuna Sagar Dam Located in Andhra Pradesh. 5. Tehri Dam Located in Uttarakhand, this dam is the highest dam in India. > कल्लनई बांध [ग्रैंड एनीकट बांध] भारत का सबसे पुराना बांध है। इसे चोल वंश के राजा करिकला ने बनवाया था। भारत में अन्य महत्वपूर्ण बांध:- 1. भाखड़ा नांगल बांध हिमाचल प्रदेश और पंजाब में स्थित यह बांध भारत का सबसे बड़ा और एशिया का दूसरा सबसे बड़ा बांध है। 2. हीराकुंड बांध ओडिशा में स्थित यह बांध दुनिया का सबसे लंबा मानव निर्मित बांध है। 3. सरदार सरोवर बांध गुजरात में स्थित यह बांध नर्मदा घाटी परियोजना का हिस्सा है। 4. नागार्जुन सागर बांध आंध्र प्रदेश में स्थित है। 5. टिहरी बांध उत्तराखंड में स्थित यह बांध भारत का सबसे ऊंचा बांध है।

questions.question: Batholiths are landforms created by? > बाथोलिथ किसके द्वारा निर्मित भू-आकृतियाँ हैं?
questions.explanation: Batholiths are landforms created by the intrusion and solidification of magma deep within the Earth's crust. They are large bodies of igneous rock that are made up of plutons, which are several kilometres in diameter. A well-known batholith is located in the Sierra Nevada range of California > बाथोलिथ पृथ्वी की पपड़ी के भीतर गहरे मैग्मा के बाहर आने और जमने से निर्मित भू-आकृतियाँ हैं। वे आग्नेय चट्टान के बड़े पिंड हैं जो प्लूटन से बने होते हैं, जो कई किलोमीटर व्यास के होते हैं। एक प्रसिद्ध बाथोलिथ कैलिफोर्निया के सिएरा नेवादा रेंज में स्थित है

questions.question: Select the wrong option in respect of the process of Saponification? > साबुनीकरण की प्रक्रिया के संबंध में गलत विकल्प का चयन करें?
options: Hydrgenation of the fats and oils take place>वसा और तेलों का हाइड्रोजनीकरण होता है।
By-Product of this process is glycerol>इस प्रक्रिया का उप-उत्पाद ग्लिसरॉल है।
Heating of fat or oil with a strong alkali is necessary >एक मजबूत क्षार के साथ वसा या तेल को गर्म करना आवश्यक है।
Soap is the main product of this process>साबुन इस प्रक्रिया का मुख्य उत्पाद है।
questions.explanation: Correct answer is option A because there is Hydrolysis of the fats and oils take place not Hydrogenation. Saponification is a chemical reaction that breaks down fats and oils to create soap and glycerol. It's also known as the soap-making process. > सही उत्तर विकल्प A है क्योंकि वसा और तेल का हाइड्रोलिसिस होता है न कि हाइड्रोजनीकरण। साबुनीकरण एक रासायनिक प्रतिक्रिया है जो साबुन और ग्लिसरॉल बनाने के लिए वसा और तेल को तोड़ती है। इसे साबुन बनाने की प्रक्रिया के रूप में भी जाना जाता है।

questions.question: Which of the following alphabet is used for Highlands [Polar area] according to Koppen's Classification? > कोपेन वर्गीकरण के अनुसार हाइलैंड्स [ध्रुवीय क्षेत्र] के लिए निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी वर्णमाला का उपयोग किया जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Koppen'sclimate classification scheme divides climates into five main climate groups: A [tropical], B [arid], C [temperate], D [continental], W [Desert] and E [polar]; small letters like w [winter dry], s [summer dry], m [mansoon] and f [rainy]. > कोपेन जलवायु वर्गीकरण योजना जलवायु को पांच मुख्य जलवायु समूहों में विभाजित करती है: A [उष्णकटिबंधीय], B [शुष्क], C [शीतोष्ण], D [महाद्वीपीय], W[रेगिस्तान] और E [ध्रुवीय]; छोटे अक्षर जैसे w [शीतकालीन शुष्क], s [ग्रीष्मकालीन शुष्क], m [मानसून] और f [बरसाती] जैसे छोटे अक्षर मे प्रदर्शित किया गया है।

questions.question: Archana Kamath is famous player of? > अर्चना कामथ किसकी प्रसिद्ध खिलाड़ी हैं?
questions.explanation: Some famous badminton players from India include Prakash Padukone, Saina Nehwal, PV Sindhu, Srikanth Kidambi, Ashwini Ponnappa, and Jwala Gutta etc. Some famouse table tennis players in India include Sharath Kamal, Manika Batra, Sreeja Akula, Archana Kamath and Ayhika Mukherjee etc. Some famouse Hockey player in India include DhyanChand, Balbir Singh, Mohammad Shahid, PR Shreejesh, Harmanpreet Singh etc. > भारत के कुछ प्रसिद्ध बैडमिंटन खिलाड़ियों में प्रकाश पादुकोण, साइना नेहवाल, पीवी सिंधु, श्रीकांत किदांबी, अश्विनी पोनप्पा और ज्वाला गुट्टा आदि शामिल हैं। भारत में कुछ प्रसिद्ध टेबल टेनिस खिलाड़ियों में शरथ कमल, मनिका बत्रा, श्रीजा अकुला, अर्चना कामथ और आहिका मुखर्जी आदि शामिल हैं। भारत में कुछ प्रसिद्ध हॉकी खिलाड़ियों में ध्यानचंद, बलबीर सिंह, मोहम्मद शाहिद, पीआर श्रीजेश, हरमनप्रीत सिंह आदि शामिल हैं।

questions.question: Which Govt. portal was launched by the Govt. of India to provide a single window for registering complaints of sexual harassment? > यौन उत्पीड़न की शिकायतों को दर्ज करने के लिए एकल खिड़की प्रदान करने के लिए भारत सरकार द्वारा कौन सा सरकारी पोर्टल शुरू किया गया था?
questions.explanation: The ‘SHe-Box’ portal is an online system that allows women to report sexual harassment at the workplace. It was launched by the Ministry of Women and Child Development to help implement the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace [Prevention, Prohibition, and Redressal] Act, 2013. > 'शी-बॉक्स' [‘SHe-Box’] पोर्टल एक ऑनलाइन प्रणाली है जो महिलाओं को कार्यस्थल पर यौन उत्पीड़न की रिपोर्ट करने की अनुमति देती है। यह महिला और बाल विकास मंत्रालय द्वारा कार्यस्थल पर महिलाओं के यौन उत्पीड़न [रोकथाम, निषेध और निवारण] अधिनियम, 2013 को लागू करने में मदद करने के लिए शुरू किया गया था।

questions.question: Chhatrapati Shivaji and Sayarabai named their son? > छत्रपति शिवाजी और सायराबाई ने अपने पुत्र का नाम रखा था?
questions.explanation: Rajaram Bhosle I was the second son of Maratha ruler Shivaji and step brother of Shambhaji who was the son of Shivaji and Saibai. > राजाराम भोसले प्रथम मराठा शासक शिवाजी के दूसरे पुत्र और शंभाजी के सौतेले भाई थे, शंभाजी शिवाजी और साईबाई के पुत्र थे।

questions.question: Which city is known as the 'Manchester of South India'? >किस शहर को 'दक्षिण भारत का मैनचेस्टर' कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Coimbatore, a city in Tamil Nadu, India, is known as the "Manchester of South India" due to its flourishing textile industry. The city shares similarities with Manchester, England, which is also renowned as a major center for cotton textiles. Coimbatore is one of the most industrialized cities in Tamil Nadu and serves as a key hub for textile production. Similarly, Ahmedabad, located in Gujarat, is referred to as the "Manchester of India" because of its significant contribution to the countries textile industry. Kanpur, in Uttar Pradesh, is also known as the "Manchester of North India" for its historical prominence in textile manufacturing. > भारत के तमिलनाडु में स्थित कोयंबटूर शहर को अपने समृद्ध कपड़ा उद्योग के कारण "दक्षिण भारत का मैनचेस्टर" कहा जाता है। यह शहर इंग्लैंड के मैनचेस्टर से मिलता-जुलता है, जो सूती वस्त्रों के लिए एक प्रमुख केंद्र के रूप में भी प्रसिद्ध है। कोयंबटूर तमिलनाडु के सबसे अधिक औद्योगिक शहरों में से एक है और कपड़ा उत्पादन के लिए एक प्रमुख केंद्र के रूप में कार्य करता है। इसी तरह, गुजरात में स्थित अहमदाबाद को देश के कपड़ा उद्योग में इसके महत्वपूर्ण योगदान के कारण "भारत का मैनचेस्टर" कहा जाता है। उत्तर प्रदेश में स्थित कानपुर को कपड़ा निर्माण में अपनी ऐतिहासिक प्रमुखता के कारण "उत्तर भारत का मैनचेस्टर" भी कहा जाता है।

questions.question: Who was the 11th President and first Muslim President to serve an entire term? > पूर्ण कार्यकाल की सेवा देने वाले 11 वें और पहले मुस्लिम राष्ट्रपति कौन थे?
options: APJ Abdul Kalam>ए पी जे अब्दुल कलाम
questions.explanation: APJ Abdul Kalam was the 11th President and first Muslim President to serve an entire term. The first President of India was Rajendra Prasad and first muslim President of India was Zakir Hussain. > एपीजे अब्दुल कलाम 11 वें राष्ट्रपति और पूरे कार्यकाल की सेवा करने वाले पहले मुस्लिम राष्ट्रपति थे। भारत के पहले राष्ट्रपति राजेंद्र प्रसाद थे और भारत के पहले मुस्लिम राष्ट्रपति जाकिर हुसैन थे।

questions.question: Unemployment, when people who are able and willing to work can't find a job? > बेरोजगारी, जब जो लोग काम करने में सक्षम और इच्छुक होते हैं उन्हें नौकरी नहीं मिल पाती है?
questions.explanation: Open unemployment is when people who are able and willing to work can't find a job. It's a visible problem that can affect individuals and society. Causes Lack of job opportunities. There aren't enough jobs for people with their skills and preferences. > खुली बेरोजगारी तब होती है जब जो लोग काम करने में सक्षम और इच्छुक होते हैं उन्हें नौकरी नहीं मिल पाती है। यह एक दृश्य समस्या है जो व्यक्तियों और समाज को प्रभावित कर सकती है। नौकरी के अवसरों की कमी का कारण बनती है। इस स्थिति मे कौशल और वरीयताओं वाले लोगों के लिए पर्याप्त नौकरियां नहीं हैं।

questions.question: Tokhu Emong festival related to which state? > तोखू इमोंग त्योहार किस राज्य से संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: Tokhu Emong festival is a post-harvest festival, the celebration include offering prayers to God for joy and prosperity. This festival is celebrated by the Lotha Nagas in the Indian state Nagaland. Other festival of Nagaland are Hornbill, Moatsu, Mim kut, Sekrenyi, Tsukheneye and Aoleang ect. > तोखू इमोंग त्योहार फसल कटाई के बाद का त्योहार है, इस उत्सव में खुशी और समृद्धि के लिए भगवान से प्रार्थना करना शामिल है। यह त्योहार भारतीय राज्य नागालैंड में लोथा नागाओं द्वारा मनाया जाता है। नागालैंड के अन्य त्योहार हॉर्नबिल, मोआत्सु, मिम कुट, सेकरेनी, त्सुखेने और एओलेंग हैं।

questions.question: Who gave the five kingdom classification? > पांच जगत वर्गीकरण किसने दिया?
questions.explanation: The five kingdom classification is a system that divides living things into five kingdoms: Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia. This classification was proposed by R.H. Whittaker in 1969. > पांच जगत वर्गीकरण एक ऐसी प्रणाली है जो जीवित चीजों को पांच वर्गों में विभाजित करती है:- मोनेरा, प्रोटिस्टा, फंगी, प्लांटे और एनिमेलिया। यह वर्गीकरण 1969 में आर एच व्हिटेकर द्वारा प्रस्तावित किया गया था

questions.question: Book ‘Night of the Scorpion’ is written by? > पुस्तक 'नाइट ऑफ द स्कॉर्पियन' किसके द्वारा लिखी गई है?
questions.explanation: Famous book ‘Night of the Scorpion’ is written by Nissim Ezekiel. Other important books:- Arvind Adiga: Selection Day, Amnesty, The White Tiger etc. Vikram Seth: A Switable Boy, the Golden Gate, Two Line: A memoir etc,. Chetan Bhagat: Half Girlfriend, One Indian Girl, One Night at the Call Centre etc,. > प्रसिद्ध पुस्तक 'नाइट ऑफ द स्कॉर्पियन' निसिम ईजेकील द्वारा लिखी गई है। अन्य महत्वपूर्ण पुस्तकें:- अरविंद अडिगा: सिलेक्शन डे, एमनेस्टी, द व्हाइट टाइगर आदि। विक्रम सेठ: ए स्विटेबल बॉय, द गोल्डन गेट, टू लाइन: ए मेमॉयर आदि। चेतन भगत: आधी प्रेमिका, एक भारतीय लड़की, एक रात कॉल सेंटर पर आदि।

questions.question: Which articles are included in Part IXA of the Constitution? > संविधान के भाग IXA में कौन से अनुच्छेद शामिल हैं?
questions.explanation: Articles 243P to 243ZG of the Constitution of India are part of Part IXA, which is titled "The Municipalities". This part was added by the 74th Amendment Act of 1992, also known as the Nagarpalika Act. The amendment came into effect on June 1, 1993. Articles 243B–243O of the Constitution of India is related to the constitution of panchayats. These articles are part of Part IX of the Constitution, which was added by the 73rd Amendment in 1992. > भारत के संविधान के अनुच्छेद 243P से 243ZG भाग IXA का हिस्सा हैं, जिसका शीर्षक "नगर पालिका" है। यह भाग 1992 के 74वें संशोधन अधिनियम द्वारा जोड़ा गया था, जिसे नगरपालिका अधिनियम के रूप में भी जाना जाता है। यह संशोधन 1 जून, 1993 को लागू हुआ। भारत के संविधान के अनुच्छेद 243B-243O पंचायतों के गठन से संबंधित हैं। ये अनुच्छेद संविधान के भाग IX का हिस्सा हैं, जिसे 1992 में 73वें संशोधन द्वारा जोड़ा गया था।

questions.question: Manas National Park located in? > मानस राष्ट्रीय उद्यान कहाँ स्थित है?
questions.explanation: Assam’s National Parks:- Kaziranga, Manas, Dibru-Saikhowa, Nameri, Dehing Patkai, and Raimona. These parks are home to a variety of wildlife, including tigers, elephants, and rhinoceroses. > असम के राष्ट्रीय उद्यान:- काजीरंगा, मानस, डिब्रू-सैखोवा, नामेरी, देहिंग पटकाई और रायमोना। ये पार्क बाघ, हाथी और गैंडे सहित विभिन्न प्रकार के वन्यजीवों के घर हैं।

questions.question: In which of the following years was the Diwani of the Bengal transferred to the East India Company? > निम्नलिखित में से किस वर्ष में बंगाल की दीवानी ईस्ट इंडिया कंपनी को हस्तांतरित की गई थी?
questions.explanation: The Diwani right of Bengal, Bihar, and Orissa was transferred to the EIC through the treaty of Allahabad which was signed on 12 August 1765 after the battle of Buxar in 1764. This treaty was signed b/w Emperor Shah Alam II and Robert Clive of EIC. > बंगाल, बिहार और उड़ीसा के दीवानी अधिकार को इलाहाबाद की संधि के माध्यम से EIC में स्थानांतरित कर दिया गया था, जिस पर 1764 में बक्सर की लड़ाई के बाद 12 अगस्त 1765 को हस्ताक्षर किए गए थे। इस संधि पर सम्राट शाह आलम द्वितीय और EIC के रॉबर्ट क्लाइव के बीच हस्ताक्षर किए गए थे।

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: Use "drank A cup of coffee." CONCEPT: Use an article ["a" or "an"] before singular countable nouns. This is because singular countable nouns require a determiner to indicate that we are referring to one specific item. Without the article, the phrase sounds incomplete and grammatically incorrect. The correct sentence would be: "Amit doesn’t usually drink coffee, but this morning he drank A cup of coffee." So, the error is in segment [C], where the noun "cup" is missing the article "a."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The employee received __________for his workplace injury.
questions.explanation: The word "compensation" refers to something given to make up for a loss, damage, or injury, making it the most appropriate choice in this context. The sentence implies that the employee was given something in return for suffering a workplace injury. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Loss -हानि – The state of losing something. • Example: "The company faced a huge loss due to the economic downturn." • Compensation -मुआवजा– Something [usually money] given to make up for a loss or injury. • Example: "The worker received compensation for the injuries sustained in the accident." • Refund -वापसी– Money returned for a purchase. • Example: "She got a refund for the defective product." • Discount -छूट – A reduction in price • Example: "The store is offering a 20% discount on all electronics." Hence, Option B, "Compensation," is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. It has been three years since I have seen him last.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Since I saw him Explanation: • The sentence "It has been three years since I have seen him last" is incorrect because when referring to a point in the past that marks the start of a duration [in this case, "three years ago"], the simple past tense ["saw"] should be used instead of the present perfect ["have seen"]. • The phrase "since I have seen him last" uses an unnecessary present perfect construction after the expression "it has been" which already implies a period of time. Improved Sentence: "It has been three years since I saw him last." Thus, the most appropriate choice is Option C - "Since I saw him."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: One cannot always choose what is in his own interest
I cannot say who of the boys was shouting.
You and Ahmed have wasted your time.
That is the first time that I have seen you.
questions.explanation: Correct Sentence: You and Ahmed have wasted your time. ✅ Explanation: • The sentence is grammatically correct because "You and Ahmed" is a compound subject, and "have" correctly agrees with the plural subject. The pronoun "your" is also appropriately used for both "you" and Ahmed. Why the other options are incorrect: [A] One cannot always choose what is in his own interest. ❌ • "His" is not consistent with the gender-neutral subject "one." It should be "one's" instead of "his." • Correct sentence: "One cannot always choose what is in one's own interest." [B] I cannot say who of the boys was shouting. ❌ • "Who" is used for people when referring to individuals, whereas "which" is used for things or to specify one item out of a set. Since "the boys" is a specific group of people, you need "which" to correctly refer to one of them. • Correct sentence: “I cannot say which of the boys was shouting.” [D] That is the first time that I have seen you. ❌ • "That" would be used to refer to something further in the past or not immediately present, which doesn't align with the intended meaning here, where you're talking about the current situation. • Correct sentence: "This is the first time I have seen you."

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: Possession is the correct spelling. • Possession [अधिकार] – The state of owning or having something. Example: "The ancient temple is in the possession of the archaeological department." • Pomegrenate [Incorrect] – Pomegranate [Correct] -अनार– A fruit with red seeds inside. Example: "She enjoys drinking fresh pomegranate juice every morning." • Sieze [Incorrect] – Seize [Correct] -पकड़ लेना – To take hold of something forcefully. Example: "The police managed to seize the stolen goods from the criminals." • Exponant [Incorrect] – Exponent [Correct] प्रतिपादक – A person who supports or promotes a belief or idea. Example: "She is a strong exponent of environmental conservation."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Most married people fall out over money.
questions.explanation: No improvement required Explanation: The phrase "fall out" means to have a disagreement or quarrel, often leading to a damaged relationship, which fits the context of the sentence correctly. • Fall out: To have a disagreement or quarrel • Example: The two friends appear to have fallen out over a minor issue. • Fall back: To retreat or withdraw. • Example: "The soldiers were forced to fall back due to heavy enemy fire." • Fall through: To fail or not happen as planned. • Example: "Our vacation plans fell through because of the bad weather." • Fall for: To be deceived by or develop romantic feelings for someone. Example: "She fell for his charm instantly." Conclusion: The correct phrase "fall out" already conveys the intended meaning, so Option D [No improvement require] is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - I've never gone out of my way to PROPITIATE people.
questions.explanation: Sol: Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Propitiate - शांत करना / तुष्ट करना - To win the favor of someone by doing something pleasing. Example: "The villagers made offerings to propitiate the gods." • Appease - शांति स्थापित करना - To calm or pacify someone, often by meeting their demands. Example: "The government tried to appease the protestors by promising reforms." • Restrain - रोकना - To hold back or prevent from acting freely. Example: "He restrained himself from making an angry comment." • Deceive - धोखा देना - To mislead or trick someone. Example: "He deceived his customers by selling fake products." • Mock - मज़ाक उड़ाना - To ridicule or make fun of someone. Example: "They mocked his accent during the speech." Conclusion: "Appease" is the most appropriate synonym for "propitiate," as both words refer to actions taken to pacify or win favour. Correct answer: [A] Appease.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. INCORPORATE
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Incorporate - सम्मिलित करना - To include or combine something as part of a whole. Example: "The new design will incorporate feedback from customers." • Exclude - बाहर करना - To leave out or not include something or someone. Example: "The policy will exclude those who fail to meet the requirements." • Submit - प्रस्तुत करना - To present or offer something formally. Example: "Students must submit their assignments by Friday." • Formal - औपचारिक - Following official rules or customs. Example: "He wore a formal suit to the meeting." • Insert - डालना - To put something inside something else. Example: "Please insert the key into the lock." Conclusion: "Exclude" is the most appropriate antonym of "incorporate," as incorporating means adding, while excluding means leaving out. Correct answer: [D] Exclude.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. She confides ________ him whenever she has a problem.
questions.explanation: The correct preposition to use with "confides" is "in." Correct Sentence: "She confides IN him whenever she has a problem." • "Confide in" means to trust someone and share secrets or personal matters with them. Example: "I always confide in my best friend when I need advice."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Pull no punches
options: To be honest and direct without holding back
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Option [C] - To be honest and direct without holding back Explanation: "Pull no punches" – "बिना किसी संकोच के सच बोलना" This idiom means to speak openly and honestly, without softening criticism or holding back one's opinion. It is often used when someone is being very straightforward, even if their words may seem harsh. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase comes from boxing, where "pulling a punch" means to deliberately hit lightly or avoid full force. If someone "pulls no punches," they are hitting with full force, both literally and metaphorically. Example: "The teacher pulled no punches while giving feedback on our projects." Hindi: "शिक्षक ने हमारी परियोजनाओं पर प्रतिक्रिया देते समय कोई संकोच नहीं किया।"

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: quality assurance procedures, the new software
is thought to be launching in the market next month.
questions.explanation: The segment containing the grammatical error is: "is thought to be launching in the market next month." Concept: • The verb phrase "is thought to be launching" is incorrect because the word "launching" is in the present participle form, which doesn't fit with the intended meaning of the sentence. • "To be" should be followed by the past participle form of the verb [in this case, "launched"] to indicate the action that is expected to be completed in the future. [To + Verb 1st form] [To + be + Verb 3rd form] Corrected Sentence: "After undergoing thorough testing and quality assurance procedures, the new software is thought to be LAUNCHED in the market next month."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. PLIGHT
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of "PLIGHT" along with its usage: • Plight – कठिन परिस्थिति – A dangerous, difficult, or unfortunate situation. Example: "The plight of the homeless during winter is heartbreaking." Now, let's analyze the given options: • Shiver – कांपना – To shake slightly due to cold or fear. Example: "He began to shiver in the freezing wind." • Difficulty – कठिनाई – A situation that is hard to deal with. Example: "She faced great difficulty in solving the puzzle." • Pledge – प्रतिज्ञा – A formal promise or commitment. Example: "He took a pledge to donate to charity every year." • Suspicious – संदिग्ध – Having doubt or distrust about something. Example: "His suspicious behaviour made them question his intentions." Hence, Option B - DIFFICULTY, is the most appropriate synonym of PLIGHT.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- Idleness is the parent of all VICE.
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of "VICE" along with its usage: • Vice – बुराई/दुर्गुण – Immoral or wicked behaviour. Example: "Gambling is often considered a serious vice." Now, let's analyze the given options: • Progress – प्रगति – Forward movement towards improvement. Example: "The country has made significant progress in technology." • Sarcasm – व्यंग्य – The use of irony to mock or convey contempt. Example: "His sarcasm often made people uncomfortable." • Virtue – सद्गुण – High moral standards or good behaviour. Example: "Honesty is considered a great virtue." • Strength – शक्ति – The quality of being physically or mentally strong. Example: "She showed great strength in overcoming adversity." Hence, Option C - VIRTUE, is the most appropriate antonym of VICE.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The black clouds show that a storm is _____________.
questions.explanation: The sentence should read: "The black clouds show that a storm is IMMINENT." This sentence means that the storm is about to happen very soon. The word "imminent" means something that is likely to occur at any moment, which fits the context perfectly. Here are the explanations for the other options: • Imminent – "आसन्न" – About to happen very soon. • Example: "The arrival of the train is imminent." • Eminent – "प्रतिष्ठित" – Famous, respected, or distinguished. • Example: "He is an eminent scientist in his field." • Emanant – "निसृत" – Flowing out or radiating from a source. • Example: "The light is emanant from the lamp." • Imanent – Incorrect spelling, no meaning in English. Correct Answer: [A] Imminent.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Pertaining to sheep
questions.explanation: Here are the explanations for all options: • Lupine – "भेड़िये से संबंधित" – Related to wolves. • Example: "His sharp features and wild hair gave him a lupine look." • Equine – "घोड़े से संबंधित" – Related to horses. • Example: "She has a passion for equine sports like horse racing." • Vulpine – "लोमड़ी जैसा या चालाक" – Related to foxes; cunning. • Example: "His vulpine nature made him a master of deception." • Ovine – "भेड़ से संबंधित" – Related to sheep. • Example: "The ovine wool industry is very profitable." Correct Answer: [D] Ovine – as it means "pertaining to sheep."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Sometimes, the best medicine for a grating issue is to sleep on it.
options: To seek support with any intelligent person
To ignore a problem and hope it goes away
To delay making a decision until the next day
To take a nap before an important task
questions.explanation: Sleep on it- सोचने के लिए रातभर का समय लेना- means to delay making a decision until the next day. It's the act of giving yourself time to think about something overnight, helping you make a more informed and thoughtful decision. Origin of this Idiom: This idiom originates from the idea that sleep helps clear the mind, so when you are faced with a tough decision, sleeping on it allows you to wake up with a fresh perspective. questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. A government by military class
questions.explanation: Explanation: A Stratocracy is a government system where the military class holds political power. The term comes from the Greek words "stratos" [army] and "kratos" [power or rule], indicating military rule. Here are the words with their Hindi and English meanings, along with usage: • Communism – "साम्यवाद" – A system where the state controls all property and resources, aiming for a classless society. • Example: "Communism aims to eliminate private ownership of land and businesses." • Oligarchy – "शाही वर्ग का शासन" – A government controlled by a small, elite group of individuals or families. • Example: "The country was ruled by an oligarchy of wealthy families." • Aristocracy – "अभिजात वर्ग" – A government ruled by a privileged class, often based on noble birth or wealth. • Example: "The aristocracy in the medieval period held all the power." • Stratocracy – "सैन्य द्वारा शासित शासन" – A form of government in which the military class holds political power. • Example: "In a stratocracy, military leaders take charge of all major decisions." Hence, the correct answer is: [D] Stratocracy – A government by military class.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. He decided to walk to the office, as the weather was pleasant. B. Walking gave him time to think, which was exactly what he needed. C. He had been feeling overwhelmed with work lately. D. The peaceful morning air helped clear his mind.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: CABD This order makes sense because: • Sentence C introduces the context, explaining that he had been feeling overwhelmed with work lately. • Sentence A follows, providing the decision he made to walk to the office due to the pleasant weather. • Sentence B explains how walking gave him time to think, which was exactly what he needed. • Sentence D concludes with how the peaceful morning air helped clear his mind, tying everything together. Final paragraph: "He had been feeling overwhelmed with work lately. He decided to walk to the office, as the weather was pleasant. Walking gave him time to think, which was exactly what he needed. The peaceful morning air helped clear his mind."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. Follow all the instructions carefully.
options: All the instructions could be followed carefully.
Let all the instructions be followed carefully.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Let all the instructions be followed carefully. Explanation: The given sentence is an imperative sentence in active voice, where the subject "You" [implied] is being asked to follow the instructions carefully. Active Voice Structure: • You [Subject] + follow [Verb] + all the instructions carefully [Object]. Passive Voice Structure: • Let [Introductory Verb] + all the instructions [Object] + be followed [Passive Verb] + carefully [Adverb]. Thus, the correct transformation is: "Let all the instructions be followed carefully."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. “Is that so?” the Sahib said cheerfully. “Well, I like village folk.”
options: The Sahib cheerfully asked if it was such and said he likes village folk.
The Sahib asked cheerfully whether that was so, adding that he liked village folk.
The Sahib told with cheer that it was so and asked if he liked village folk.
The Sahib was asking cheerfully if it was so and added he liked village folk.
questions.explanation: Explanation: In indirect speech, we need to transform the direct speech in a way that accurately represents the speaker’s tone, meaning, and structure. • The sentence “Is that so?” is a question in direct speech. In indirect speech, this question becomes "whether that was so" to convey the same meaning. • The second part of the sentence “Well, I like village folk.” is a statement in direct speech, which becomes "adding that he liked village folk" in indirect speech. • We maintain the cheerfulness by using the adverb "cheerfully" in the indirect speech as well. Thus, the correct transformation is: "The Sahib asked cheerfully whether that was so, adding that he liked village folk."

questions.question: Grameen is now one of the world’s biggest microfinance organizations. It began by _____1_____ small loans to underprivileged entrepreneurs in Bangladesh. questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Escape Explanation - The verb "escape" means to break free or get away from something, often something unpleasant or restrictive. In this context, it aligns with Grameen's goal of helping individuals overcome poverty. Let's see why other options are wrong: • Indulge: This option implies giving in to a desire or pleasure, often excessively. It doesn't align with Grameen's goal and has a negative connotation. • Aggravate: This option means making a problem worse, which is the opposite of Grameen's goal. • Intensify: This option means making something stronger or more extreme, which also doesn't align with Grameen's goal.
